subtasks.description: Query SAM contract database for Slack license renewal records, extracting contract start/end dates, renewal history, seat counts, and pricing terms
Query consumption data warehouse for Slack usage metrics over time: monthly active users, daily active users, storage utilization, and any available API rate limits or feature usage

Reconcile SAM renewal dates with consumption trends to identify usage patterns relative to renewal windows. Classify renewal posture (early renewal, at-risk, declining, growth trajectory) and recommend renewal strategy based on alignment between contracted seats and actual usage

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks minimum: (1) SAM contract retrieval (mongo_query, mid) handles complex renewal record filtering; (2) consumption trend query (sql_gen, mid) aggregates usage metrics across time periods; (3) cross_recon (frontier) performs multi-signal reconciliation of renewal dates against usage trends, identifies trajectory patterns, classifies renewal posture, and generates strategy recommendations—this reconciliation requires inferential reasoning across two data sources with temporal alignment and business logic, warranting frontier tier. Frontier tier on the final subtask justified because it must synthesize temporal, financial, and usage signals to produce actionable renewal trajectory insights rather than simple alignment reporting.

output.sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date) AS month, cf.product, cf.event_type, COUNT(DISTINCT cf.user_id) AS monthly_active_users, AVG(cf.daily_active_seats) AS avg_daily_active_seats, MAX(cf.daily_active_seats) AS peak_daily_active_seats, SUM(cf.amount_usd) AS total_spend_usd, COUNT(cf.event_id) AS event_count, AVG(cf.amount_usd) AS avg_event_amount_usd FROM consumption_facts cf WHERE LOWER(cf.product) LIKE '%slack%' AND cf.event_date >= '2023-01-01' GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', cf.event_date), cf.product, cf.event_type ORDER BY month ASC, cf.event_type
